# feat(m6): convert all 8 exercises to R10 directory structure

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `f7f9960f5935` — feat(m6): convert all 8 exercises to R10 directory structure

**Body**:

Each exercise split into per-technique files following the 5-phase
narrative: Theory → Build → Train → Visualise → Apply.

Exercises:
  ex_1: 6 files (zero-shot, few-shot, CoT, zero-shot CoT, self-consistency, structured)
  ex_2: 6 files (LoRA, adapter, landscape, merging, quantisation, alignment pipeline)
  ex_3: 4 files (preference data, DPO loss, DPO training, GRPO + judge)
  ex_4: 5 files (chunking, dense, BM25, hybrid RRF, rerank + pipeline)
  ex_5: 4 files (ReAct, cost budget, structured, critic)
  ex_6: 5 files (supervisor-worker, sequential, parallel router, MCP, memory + security)
  ex_7: 4 files (org compile, envelopes, budget access, runtime audit)
  ex_8: 5 files (adapter loading, governance, multichannel, drift, compliance audit)

Total: 39 technique files × 3 formats (solutions/local/colab) + 8 shared modules.

Helpers extracted to `shared/mlfp06/ex_N.py` for `uv sync`-based imports.
Old monolithic exercise files removed.

Also updates:
- exercise-standards.md with R10 directory section
- scripts/py_to_notebook.py to understand R10 subdirectories

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
